scope_summary.problem: → requirements.md § R1-R7: Scheduler lacks push receiver endpoint, K8s CronJob backend, schedule monitoring, and mode selection logic.
scope_summary.success_criteria: → requirements.md § R1: Push receiver HTTP endpoint mounted on existing server. → pre_clarifications Q1: axum Router mounted on cclab server. → pre_clarifications Q2: K8s CronJob pods POST to push receiver. → pre_clarifications Q3: Metrics + webhook alerting for missed schedules. → pre_clarifications Q4: HMAC shared secret for K8s auth.

boundary.in_scope: → spec_plan: push-receiver (HTTP endpoint + auth), k8s-cronjob-backend (SchedulerBackend impl), schedule-monitor (expected vs actual tracking + metrics + webhook), scheduler-mode-selection (tick loop vs push receiver start logic).
boundary.out_of_scope: Existing Ion/Memory/CloudScheduler backends (no changes). Broker implementations (no changes). Worker/executor logic (no changes). cclab-server framework changes (push receiver is a mountable Router, not a server change).
boundary.constraints: cclab-queue is a library — push receiver must be an axum Router that consuming apps mount. No standalone HTTP server. Metrics follow existing TaskMetrics pattern in metrics.rs (Prometheus counters/histograms via prometheus crate).
